records.m_pText: Get This Over With
Alright, let’s hurry up$nand get this over with.$nAwaken the crystals or whatever.
Get this over with?
She’s right, [PCF2].$nWe’re trying to close the Great—
I know we’re trying to close the$nGreat Chasm!
I know because we’ve entered the$nHoly Pillar three times already!
S-sorry.
You’re sorry, you say?
I don’t want apologies from you,$nAiry!
Just hurry up and do something$nabout the Great Chasm, already!
*sniffle*$nI’m sorry.
[PCF2], believe me,$nI know how you feel.
But look, you see that?
With the sea gone putrid,$nfishermen can’t put out$nto sea to earn a living.
And people await family members$nunable to sail home from$nfaraway lands.
Let’s clear the taint from the$nsea for their sake, okay?
Then there’s the wind in Ancheim.
And the seething magma in Eisenberg.
…You people are so…so softhearted.
Come, now, [PCF2].$nLet us get this over with,$nso we may help them!
Right, let’s go!
Galette Saucisse
Oh, there you are, Datz.
Yep, came to relieve Zatz.
Or…are you really here for$nEisenberg’s famous sausages$nand ham…
…all washed down with a hearty$nblack beer?
Bah ha ha!$nYou got me there!
You obviously know what’s good,$n[PCM2]. Care to join me?
Unfortunately, I’ve some business$nwith Eleanor.
You don’t say?
Yes, well you see, we picked up a$nheap of buckwheat seeds while$nwe were in Florem.
Figured buckwheat would be a$nperfect crop for the barren$nsoil here.
And it would help the food$nsituation, too.
[PCM2], I’m so grateful you’re$nso concerned for this land.
I’m blessed with wonderful friends!
If you need to thank someone,$nit should be [PCF2].
[PCF2]?
She overheard the proprietress$ntalking to herself.
What did she say?
Apparently she was mumbling$nsomething about taking$nEisenberg’s famous sausages…
And wrapping it in a crepe made$nfrom Florem buckwheat to make$na delicious galette saucisse.
[PCF2] couldn’t stop thinking$nabout it after that, and invested$nin a heap of buckwheat seeds.
She realized that buckwheat loses its$nflavor quickly, so she decided it$nwould have to be grown locally.
Oh ho!$nShe’s got an amazing mind, that one!
That she does. But what do you expect$nfrom the only daughter of the$nduchy’s grand marshal?
Several years from now, I’ll wager$nEisenberg will have one more$nfamous dish to add to the list.
You’re still here?$nHurry up and deliver those$nseeds to Eleanor!
Yes, ma’am.$n…Oh, and Datz?
Whatever you do, don’t mention to her$nthat it would’ve been easier$nto ship sausages to Florem.
Ah, heh heh…$nThanks for the tip…
A Never-Ending Cycle
I was wondering…
Yes?
What if the Great Chasm existed$nlong before we were ever born?
…And by that, you mean…?
I mean, what if the same events$nhave been happening over and$nover thousands—even tens of$nthousands of times?
And if so, how long must we keep$ntrying to close the Great Chasm?
After all, it’s as if$nour efforts are—
…Forgive me.
It’s just, I can’t help but wonder$nwhen I look upon the Great Chasm.
Truth be told, [PCM1],$nI understand how you feel.
But I shall never forget how you$nsaved me on that hill overlooking$nthe chasm that day.
You took my hand with no regard$nfor the peril you faced.
Of that, I am certain.
So, let us believe. Let us believe$nin this path we walk together.
…You’re right.
Rest for the Weary
Whew… The first Rite of Awakening$nwent over without a hitch.
You seem to be getting quite$nused to this, [PCF1].
Well, practice makes perfect,$ndoes it not? And I have had lots$nof practice by now…
But are you alright?$nYou look so tired every time.
I shall be fine, for I feel that I$nhave finally begun to grasp the$nknack to this rite.
The knack, huh?
If only we could say the same of$nthose monsters that possess$nthe crystals…
I can’t help thinking they’re$ngetting more powerful each time.
You, too, [PCM2]?$nI was thinking the same thing.
We’ve battled them again and again,$nlearning their habits and tendencies,$nbut each time it’s more difficult$nthan the last.
Glad it’s not only me, [PCF2].$nHow about you, [PCM1]?
Shhh!$nLooks like [PCF1] fell asleep.
Ha ha, you’re right.
Let’s let her get a little sleep.
Yes, she needs some rest.
Time for a Rest
Whew…
Are you alright, [PCF1]?
Yes…I am fine. Just a bit weary.
I’m afraid you won’t last much$nlonger if you keep going on like$nthis.
He’s right. Perhaps you should$npace yourself a bit.
No, the crystals must be awakened$nwith the utmost haste.
For only then shall we dispel the$ndarkness, close the Great Chasm,$nand render aid to [PCM1].
[PCF1]…
But what harm would there be in$ngetting a little rest?
Huh…? But we have to awaken$nthe crystals, then enter the$nHoly Pillar before—
You’re right, [PCF2].$n[PCF1]’s well-being is our$nchief concern right now.
I’m worried about her, too,$nyou know!
I mean, she’s a vestal of the$ncrystals and I’m a cryst-fairy,$nso of course I’m concerned!
Airy, we’re just worried about$n[PCF1] right now…
Not vestals or fairies or whatever!$nNow, time to get some rest. Okay?
[PCM1]…
The leader speaks.$nI suppose we must follow.
[PCM1]? Our leader?$nWhen did that happen?
When doesn’t matter.$nAre you opposed, [PCF2]?
Nah, I’m all for it!$nLet’s rest up.
Next Time
Whew, that’s that…
But I wanted to ask you$nsomething, Airy.
What, [PCM1]?$nThe rite was completed$nwithout a hitch, so…
Yes, I know. But I was$nwondering…
How many crystals must we$nawaken?
How many times must we enter$nthe Holy Pillar?
And how many worlds must$nwe cross into?
It seems never-ending.
I’ve already lost count, to be$nhonest.
Oh, um, well—
That last crystal we awakened$nwas the fifteenth.
We’ve entered the Holy Pillar$nthree times now.
Since we first met…
…this marks the fourth world$nwe have crossed into.
Oh… You people sure have good$nmemories.
It’s simply a matter of counting.
And believing that next time will$nbe the one to end it all!
Yes. And Airy, I trust next time$nwill be the one!
…Oh, yes, yes! Of course!
The Fourth Time
We’ve now awakened all four$ncrystals in this world.
That marks the sixteenth since$nwe first began.
And once again, the Holy Pillar$nmust have appeared in the south sea.
The fourth Holy Pillar…
Yes, and I hope this$nwill be last one!$nRight, Airy?
Yes…of course.
This time will be the one!
…I hope you’re right.
Now, the only thing left is to deal with$nthat problem once we get back.
Once we get back?$nTo Grandship?
What, is she in need of repair again?
Yes, there’s that,$nbut Datz and Zatz$nare handling the repairs.
And I couldn’t be happier with$ntheir work so far.
Then what problem are you$ntalking about?
The proprietress…
You know, how to get her down to the$nhold when we enter the Holy Pillar.
Ah! That is a problem.$nA very difficult one, too.
I remember the fuss she$nmade the last time.
Datz ended up having to pick her$nup and carry her down there.
And just the other day, Datz and$nZatz were debating something.$nI’m not sure what, but I’d wager$nit was about the proprietress.
I wonder what the plan is.$nOoh, I can’t wait to find out!
Ha ha, well then, I suppose the$nproprietress is in good hands.
That leaves us to head on into$nthe Holy Pillar!
What are we waiting for?
Let us go!
Thanks for the Eats
Oh, I see you’ve become quite$nthe meat eater now, [PCF1].
Yes, I can eat meat now,$njust not too much.
When we first met, you couldn’t$neat fish, let alone meat.
That is true.
When was it that you first$nstarted to eat fish and meat?
It wasn’t the first world.$nShe still couldn’t eat it then.
Perhaps it was from the third world?
Ha ha ha… In truth, it was$nsince the first world.
Really!?$nWhen, when!?
It was what [PCM2] said on our$nway from Florem to Eisenberg.
What did he say?
Some time after we left$nGrandship, our provisions$nstarted to grow low, and—
Yes, I remember that!$nWhat an awful experience.
We all dangled lines and hooks$noverboard all day long.
Yet we barely caught a thing.
And even when we did, [PCF1]$ncouldn’t eat it, anyway.
We couldn’t even harvest seaweed$nbecause we were so far out to sea.
Remember that one night where$nwe split that little herring we$ncaught amongst ourselves?
At that time, partially because$nof what had happened to Olivia,$nI had closed my heart to you all…
I will never forget this.
I did not eat a bite of the grilled$nEisen flounder that you had$nprepared for me, [PCM1].
That is when [PCM2]$nsaid to me…
“[PCF1], who do you thank$nwhen food is put on the table?”
And he scolded me, saying it was$nrude to not eat what [PCM1]$nhad made for me.
That was the first time I thought of it$nthat way, but even more surprising was$nwhat [PCM2] said next.
“The word ‘thanks’ is not only for$n[PCM1], who prepared$nyour meal.”
“It’s also for the life that was$nsacrificed so that you could eat.”
That was quite a shock to me—$nsaying “thank you” for$na life taken.
I had always avoided eating meat and$nfish because I associated it$nwith taking a life.
But [PCM2] opened my eyes.
Everything I consumed, whether$ngrain, vegetable, even tea, was$nonce alive.
But if I were to say my thanks to$nall life that had to be sacrificed$nso that I could eat and live…
Then there’s no reason you$ncan’t eat meat and fish?
Yes, exactly.
That’s not at all like$n[PCM2] to say something$nas profound as that.
I learned that from$nthe grand marshal.
You were there, too, [PCF2].$nYou must have heard what he said.
Really? Wait…$nOh!
Maybe I remember? Err…
Magnificent Horns
Those horns on that sheep over$nthere are quite large.
Ooh, they’re absolutely$nmagnificent!
Oh, but aren’t there sheep that$ndon’t have any horns at all?
Allow me! [PCM1] may be an$nexpert what with his previous life$nas a sheepherder, but I know a$nthing or two as well.
You see, depending on the$nspecies, there are sheep with$nhorns and sheep without.
And with some species,$nonly the rams have horns.
As for horn shapes, they vary.$nFor example, some are shaped$nlike a spiral, and others like a$ncorkscrew.
Interesting…
I find that animal encyclopedia$nyou keep glancing at rather$ninteresting, too.
But since you have been most$ninformative, I shall let it pass$nthis time!
Ha ha ha! Lucky me!$nMy stock just went up today!
But as I said, [PCM1] is the$nexpert here, so I defer to$nhis knowledge.
Umm…
Sorry to disappoint everyone,$nbut that’s not a sheep.$nIt’s a goat.
The Great Brussels Sprouts Giveaway
Everyone!
You simply must hear this!$nThat sage is just terrible!
Sage Yulyana?$nWhat on earth happened?
He pestered me to cook him a meal$nuntil I couldn’t stand it anymore.
I gave in and spent half a day in the$nkitchen preparing a roast$nbitter melon rice bowl!
(Ack, not that…)
And do you know what the sage$ndid with all my hard work!?
He fed it to a dog when$nI wasn’t looking!
Actually, that dog left it$nfor a cat to eat.
Yes, and then that cat$nleft it to a crow.
